title: Top US official resigns over Trump's war, says Iran posed no imminent threat

title: Iran to hold funeral for slain security chief as it vows vengeance

title: Iran missile fire kills two in central Israel

A previously unknown organization called the Islamic Movement of the Companions of the Right claimed responsibility for an explosion that targeted a synagogue in Liège, Belgium, and another unconfirmed attack at an unknown location in Greece this week. It is unclear if the organization is authentic, but recent Iranian threats aimed at European countries over the war could indicate a connection to the Islamic Republic.

description: Senegal was on Tuesday stripped of its Africa Cup of Nations title and Morocco declared champion, after the CAF appeal board ruled that the Senegalese team had forfeited the final when its players walked off the pitch before the match was over. In its ruling, the appeal board judges handed Morocco a 3-0 win.

Ali Larijani was reported to have died in an Israeli airstrike on Tuesday.

title: Iran war may force US to shift missile defenses from South Korea, Seoul says
description: The U.S. Army operates only a small number of THAAD batteries across the world, meaning a deployment can affect multiple theaters.

title: US has destroyed entire class of Iranian warships, CENTCOM commander says
description: The U.S. had struck more than 60 Iranian ships, according to U.S. Central Command Commander Adm. Brad Cooper.

title: Amid US military actions, White House struggles to explain how Iran war will end
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Tuesday told reporters it’s up to Trump “whether it’s the beginning, the middle or the end” of the war.

title: ‘Radiant’ mother from Kentucky was among 6 US service members killed in air crash in Iraq
description: The KC-135 was supporting operations against Iran on Thursday in “friendly” airspace when an unspecified incident involving another aircraft occurred.

title: Who will win the race to become Paris mayor?
description: The Paris mayoral race narrowed on Monday as a former minister hoping to wrest control of the French capital from the left sealed a deal to join forces with a fellow right-winger for the second-round run-off. France 24's Marc Perelman takes a look at how things might go when France heads to the polls this Sunday to choose not only the capital's next mayor but vote in local elections across the country.

title: Israel strikes Beirut as number of displaced mounts
description: Israeli airstrikes rained down on three neighbourhoods in Beirut early Tuesday, Lebanese state media reported, as Lebanon said more than one million people had been displaced in two weeks of fighting.

title: Larijani's death would be a 'significant blow' but may not deter Iran
description: Israel said Tuesday it had killed Iran's powerful national security chief, Ali Larijani, in what would be a huge blow to the Islamic republic, but probably not one that would deter Tehran from continuing the war against Israel and the US, says France 2''s correspondent in the Iranian capital Reza Sayah.

title: Fearing Israeli strikes, a Druze village screens for Hezbollah militants among Lebanon’s displaced
description: In the predominantly Druze city of Aley, located on Mount Lebanon, authorities scrupulously check the identity documents of the many displaced who seek refuge there. They hope the measures will reassure a population fearful of inciting Israeli strikes because of the potential presence of Hezbollah militants among the arrivals fleeing southern Lebanon.

title: REPLAY: Trump accuses NATO of 'foolish mistake' over Strait of Hormuz
description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday said the United States ​had been informed by most of its NATO allies that they did not want to get involved with the country's military ​operation ‌in Iran, a move he described ⁠as a "very foolish mistake". Trump's remarks came as he hosted Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin in the Oval Office to mark St Patrick's Day.

title: 'Misguided' military operation: Trump’s appeal for Hormuz support draws no commitments from allies
description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Peter Trubowitz, Professor of International Relations and Director of the Phelan US Centre at the London School of Economics. According to Professor Trubowitz, what we are witnessing is a dynamic in which both sides are trying to expand the scope of the conflict in order to gain leverage. Iran has broadened the battlefield by targeting regional actors and threatening the maritime chokepoint. All the while, the U

title: 'I tell my children it’s just thunder': How ordinary Iranians are dealing with war
description: As US and Israeli airstrikes continue to pound Iran, many residents there live in fear, choosing to limit outings as much as possible. France 24's Carys Garland and Ludovic de Foucaud take a look at how one family are trying as best they can to continue life as normal amid the constant threat of bombardments.

title: Pakistan denies it targeted Kabul hospital, says it hit 'terrorist' targets
description: Pakistan on Monday said it had carried out precision strikes on "military installations and terrorist support infrastructure" in Kabul but denied Afghan claims that strikes hit a drug treatment centre, killing hundreds of civilians.

title: Top US counterterrorism official resigns over Iran war
description: The director of the U.S. National Counterterrorism Center, Joe Kent, has announced his resignation, saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war in Iran. Kent said on social media that Iran “posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.”

title: What have US allies and China said about sending ships to the Strait of Hormuz?
description: US allies have either pushed back or remained cautious on Trump's initial request to send ships to the crucial oil route.

title: Video: Man rescued from under rubble after attack in Iran
description: A man was rescued from under the rubble of a destroyed building in Iran following an Israeli strike.

title: Israel carrying out ‘mass expulsion of Palestinians’ in West Bank, UN warns
description: UN rights office says more than 36,000 Palestinians forcibly displaced in a year due to Israeli settler, army violence.

title: Why Iranians are taking to Tehran’s streets during war
description: As bombs fall and millions are displaced, protests grow in Iran. Could this war be strengthening Iran’s resolve?

title: Video: What we know about Israel’s assault on southern Lebanon
description: The Israeli military has launched waves of air raids across southern Lebanon.

title: Drones expand the deadly front lines of Sudan’s devastating war
description: The United Nations is warning that escalating drone warfare in Sudan has killed more than 200 people in over a week.

title: What the Iran war looks like from the occupied West Bank
description: Iran missile shrapnel falls on West Bank Palestinians as settler attacks, Israeli raids and restrictions continue.
